data.title: Uber expands AI data platform to train smart agents
data.description:
• Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) is expanding its AI data services arm, Uber AI Solutions, Uber AI Solutions, by offering its technology platform to support AI labs and businesses globally.
  • The new offerings include customized data solutions for building smarter AI models and agents, global digital task networks, and tools to help companies build and test AI models more efficiently.
  • “We’re bringing together Uber’s platform, people, and AI systems to help other organizations build smarter AI more quickly,” Megha Yethadka, head of Uber AI Solutions, said in a statement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Accenture in charts: FQ3 revenue rises Y/Y and Q/Q across all industry groups
Emeren to go private in $2/ADS deal
data.description:
Emeren (NYSE:SOL) +6.7% pre-market Friday after saying it agreed to be taken private by Shurya Vitra Ltd. at US$0.20 per ordinary share, or US$2.00 per American Depositary Share; each ADS represents 10 shares.

Emeren said Chairman Himanshu Shah has committed to making an equity contribution to the buyer solely for the purpose of funding the merger consideration.

As of March 31, Emeren (NYSE:SOL) owned and operated IPP assets comprising ~293 MW of solar PV and 61 MWh of storage, with projects under development in Europe, the U.S. and China, according to its latest financial report.

data.title: GXO names Patrick Kelleher as CEO
data.description:
• GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) Friday announced the appointment of Patrick Kelleher as its new chief executive officer, effective August 19, 2025.

  • Kelleher brings 33 years of global supply chain experience, strategic leadership, and operational excellence to GXO, having held senior executive roles at DHL Supply Chain, a division of Deutsche Post DHL Group.

  • Most recently, he served as CEO, North America, where he oversaw significant growth and operational improvements across the business.

  • Kelleher will be based at GXO’s (NYSE:GXO) global headquarters in Greenwich, Connecticut. He will succeed Malcolm Wilson, who is retiring after a successful tenure leading GXO’s global growth strategy.

data.title: Accenture stock dips as new bookings fall 6% in Q3
data.description:
Accenture (NYSE:ACN) shares dipped nearly 4% premarket on Friday after the professional services company reported its fiscal third quarter results, highlighting an ongoing decline in new bookings.

The Irish company reported revenue of $17.7B for the quarter ended May 31 that beat analysts estimates by $380M. However, new bookings of $19.7B fell of 6% in U.S. dollars and 7% in local currency.

Generative AI new bookings amounted to $1.5B.

The company is facing challenges from a sluggish U.S. federal contracting landscape, as the Trump administration has slowed new awards and reduced existing agreements in an effort to curb federal spending.

GAAP earnings per share came at $3.49 that also topped Street estimates.

The firm updated its fiscal 2025 outlook, now expecting full-year revenue growth to be 6% to 7% in local currency, compared to +5%-+7% previously, and diluted earnings per share of $12.77 to $12.89 vs. $12.55 to $12.79 previously.

Accenture (NYSE:ACN) also lifted its free cash flow outlook to be in the range of $9B to $9.7B.

Related stocks: Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH), EPAM Systems (EPAM) and Infosys (INFY).

data.title: CarMax GAAP EPS of $1.38 beats by $0.21, revenue of $7.55B beats by $20M
data.description:
• CarMax press release (NYSE:KMX): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.38 beats by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $7.55B (+6.2% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Sales. Combined retail and wholesale used vehicle unit sales were 379,727, an increase of 5.8% from the prior year’s first quarter.
  • Retail used unit sales increased 9.0% and comparable store used unit sales increased 8.1%; wholesale units increased 1.2%.
  • Bought 336,000 vehicles from consumers and dealers, an increase of 7.2%.
    • 288,000 vehicles were purchased from consumers, up 3.3%
    • 48,000 vehicles were purchased through dealers, up 38.4%

data.title: European markets rise, Eutelsat shares jump 22% following French government capital injection
data.description:
Update: This article has been updated to reflect market movement as of 6.52am ET.

London (UKX) +0.44%

UK retail sales fall sharply in May.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.88%

France (CAC:IND)  +0.51%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.48% on Friday, looking to erase some of the week’s losses, as most sectors traded in green, while oil and gas shares fell.

Eutelsat (OTCPK:EUTLF) shares rose 22% after the European satellite operator announced a 1.35 billion euro ($1.55 billion) capital raise backed by the French state and other shareholders.

Investors, meanwhile, kept a close eye on the conflict in the Middle East between Iran and Israel, and potential direct U.S. involvement.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up less than 1 basis point at 4.42%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.53%.

The UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 4.55%.

Coming up in the session: Canada retail sales. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Accenture GAAP EPS of $3.49 beats by $0.17, revenue of $17.7B beats by $380M
data.description:
• Accenture press release (NYSE:ACN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $3.49 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $17.7B (+7.5% Y/Y) beats by $380M.
  • New bookings of $19.7 billion, a decrease of 6% in U.S. dollars and 7% in local currency
  • Generative AI new bookings of $1.5 billion
  • Quarterly cash dividend of $1.48 per share, representing a 15% increase; repurchases or redemptions of 6.0 million shares for a total of $1.8 billion

  • Fiscal 2025 Business Outlook Highlights

    • Company now expects full-year revenue growth to be 6% to 7% in local currency
    • Updates foreign exchange impact to positive 0.2%
    • Now expects operating margin to be 15.6%, an expansion of 10 basis points over adjusted operating margin
    • Now expects diluted earnings per share to be in the range of $12.77 to $12.89
    • Raises free cash flow to be in the range of $9.0 billion to $9.7 billion

data.title: KBR reports TRANSCOM terminates HomeSafe's role in Global Household Goods contract
Everything Blockchain eyes $10M strategic bet on SOL, XRP, SUI, TAO & HYPE
data.description:
Everything Blockchain (OTCPK:EBZT) Friday announced its strategic plan to deploy $10 million into five of the fastest growing blockchain networks—Solana (SOL), XRP, Sui (SUI), Bittensor (TAO), and Hyperliquid (HYPE), positioning ahead of the anticipated institutional crypto wave.

This strategy positions EBZT as the first U.S. public equity to implement a multi-token staking treasury focused on blockchain networks seeking institutional adoption.

"While Bitcoin grabbed headlines, the real money is flowing into the blockchain networks powering tomorrow's financial infrastructure," said CEO Arthur Rozenberg.

"EBZT shareholders are getting front-row seats to the biggest institutional crypto shift since Bitcoin ETFs launched but this time, we're there first."
